task.title: Adding the dependencies of a program to an MVS subproject

titlealts.navtitle: Adding the dependencies of a program to an MVS subproject

task.shortdesc: You might want to organize your MVS subproject to include all of the files that are required for a program. You can automatically add the dependencies of a COBOL or PL/I program in your MVS subproject.

taskbody.prereq:
You must first add a COBOL or PL/I program to an MVS subproject, and your program must not contain any syntax errors.
If your program depends on a file that cannot be found, an error message is returned and the file is skipped. To ensure that files are not skipped, run a local syntax check on your program. If the syntax check does not produce any errors, then all the dependencies can be located and no files are skipped.

step.cmd: Right-click the COBOL or PL/I program in the z/OS Projects view.
step.info: The Show Dependencies action is not supported for sequential data sets.

step.cmd: Click Show Dependencies in the menu.
step.stepresult: A window opens, listing the dependencies for the selected program.If the subproject you are working with belongs to a host-based project, you can only display the dependencies. You cannot add dependencies to a host-based subproject.

step.cmd: Select the dependencies that you want to add to your subproject in the list box.
Select the Add selected to subproject check box.

step.stepresult: The selected dependencies are added to your MVS subproject.

link.linktext: Determining the source of generated JCL
